DAGMA for linear SEMs

Source: R/causalDeepNet.R
dagmaLinear.Rd

R translation of DAGMA's linear optimizer (DagmaLinear) for "l2" and "logistic" losses.

Usage

dagmaLinear(
  X,
  loss_type = c("l2", "logistic"),
  lambda1 = 0.03,
  w_threshold = 0.3,
  T = 5L,
  mu_init = 1,
  mu_factor = 0.1,
  s = c(1, 0.9, 0.8, 0.7, 0.6),
  warm_iter = 30000,
  max_iter = 60000,
  lr = 3e-04,
  checkpoint = 1000L,
  beta_1 = 0.99,
  beta_2 = 0.999,
  exclude_edges = NULL,
  include_edges = NULL,
  tol = 1e-06,
  verbose = FALSE
)

Arguments

X

Numeric matrix/data.frame of shape \(n \times d\).

loss_type

One of "l2" or "logistic".

lambda1

L1 penalty on adjacency.

w_threshold

Threshold for pruning small edges.

T

Number of outer DAGMA iterations.

mu_init

Initial mu.

mu_factor

Multiplicative decay for mu.

s

Scalar or vector controlling M-matrix domain.

warm_iter

Inner iterations for \(t < T\).

max_iter

Inner iterations for \(t = T\).

lr

Adam learning rate.

checkpoint

Convergence check interval.

beta_1

Adam beta1.

beta_2

Adam beta2.

exclude_edges

Optional two-column matrix/data.frame of 1-based edges to force zero.

include_edges

Optional two-column matrix/data.frame of 1-based edges to encourage inclusion.

tol

Relative objective tolerance.

verbose

Print optimization diagnostics.

Value

Object of class dagma with weighted adjacency in $adjacency.

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# dagmaLinear(...)
} # }
